postCopy.instagram: A body in a shipping container. A crane operator, a customs agent, and a port rat walk into a murder scene. 🚢🔍 Case #046 — Dead Weight. Play free at thedailywhodunit.com
postCopy.tiktok: Free daily murder mystery game: someone died at the Rotterdam docks in 2011 and the weapon was whatever was closest to hand. Dark, funny, and surprisingly complicated. Daily deduction puzzle — thedailywhodunit.com

marketing.imagePrompt: Rotterdam container port at night in 2011, towering stacks of shipping containers in red, blue and rust, a single floodlight casting harsh white light across a wet concrete quay. A yellow hard hat lies on the ground beside an open container door. A forklift sits abandoned in the middle distance. Dark sky, sodium light, cold North Sea mist. Gritty and cinematic, style of Edward Burtynsky meets Michael Mann.
marketing.animationPrompt: Slow tracking shot along a Rotterdam container quay at night, mist rolling in off the water. Floodlights flicker on one by one. The camera finds an open container door, light spilling out. A yellow hard hat rolls slowly across the wet concrete and stops. Cut to the container interior — shadows, then darkness. Fade to black.

rookie.title: Dead Weight
rookie.flavor: Viktor Haus, a freelance cargo inspector, was found dead inside Container 7-Delta at the Europoort terminal just before the night shift ended. The container held nothing but packing foam and a very inconveniently placed body. Three port workers were on that section of the quay. Each one has a reason they shouldn't have been there.
rookie.emptyState: Container 7-Delta. One body. Three people who claim they were somewhere else.

items.name: Dirk Houten
items.detail: Night crane operator — officially on a meal break when it happened.

items.name: Marta Beel
items.detail: Customs clearance officer — processing Container 7-Delta's manifest.

items.name: Sven Laak
items.detail: Port maintenance worker — fixing a hydraulic line two rows over.

items.name: Container 7-Delta
items.detail: Where Viktor's body was found — open door, no lock damage.

items.name: The Quayside Office
items.detail: Marta's assigned workspace for the night shift.

items.name: The Maintenance Bay
items.detail: Sven's logged work location for the evening.

items.name: Steel Cargo Hook
items.detail: A loose rigging hook found near the body — blunt force trauma.

items.name: Packing Foam Block
items.detail: A dense foam block — suffocation, no external marks.

items.name: Manifest Clipboard
items.detail: A heavy metal-backed clipboard — found wiped near the door.

clues.heading: The Hook's Origin
clues.text: The steel cargo hook found beside Viktor's body is traced to Crane 7 — Dirk Houten's assigned crane for the night shift. Hooks from Crane 7 are identifiable by a yellow paint stripe on the shackle pin. This hook should have been secured in the crane cab during Dirk's meal break.
clues.alone: The murder weapon came from Dirk Houten's crane — it was not supposed to be on the container floor.

links.insight: The cargo hook from Dirk's crane found at the scene and the crane cab found unlocked during the meal break confirm Dirk removed the hook before his break — placing it at the scene in advance of the killing.

clues.heading: The Blow Direction
clues.text: The coroner confirms Viktor died from a single blow to the side of the head — delivered from the left, while Viktor faced the open container door. The blow required moderate force and a weapon close to hand. Viktor showed no signs of a struggle — he did not see it coming.
clues.alone: Viktor was struck from the left by someone he trusted or did not expect — no struggle, single opportunistic blow.

links.insight: Viktor struck from the left with no struggle and Marta Beel's confirmed presence in Container Row 7 that evening confirm she was present and Viktor had no reason to be wary of her approach.

clues.heading: Sven's Hydraulic Job
clues.text: The maintenance log for the night confirms Sven Laak's hydraulic repair job on Row 9 — two container rows from 7-Delta. The repair required a specialised torque wrench signed out from the maintenance bay at 21:15 and returned at 23:40. A forklift driver passing Row 9 at 22:30 confirms seeing Sven there, covered in hydraulic fluid.
clues.alone: Sven was physically present at Row 9 during the murder window — two rows away, covered in fluid, with no opportunity to reach Container 7-Delta undetected.

links.insight: Sven confirmed at Row 9 during the murder window and Dirk's crane log showing his cab was accessed at 22:15 during his meal break together confirm Dirk left the meal room and visited the crane before the body was found.

clues.heading: Viktor's Inspection Report
clues.text: Viktor Haus's inspection notes for the evening show Container 7-Delta was flagged for a discrepancy — the manifest listed forty crates but the container held thirty-eight. Viktor had noted this and written confirm with clearance officer — possible misdeclaration. Marta Beel was the assigned clearance officer.
clues.alone: Viktor had found a discrepancy in Marta's manifest and was about to report it — she had direct professional exposure to his findings.

links.insight: Viktor's flagged discrepancy in Marta's manifest and the port authority notification drafted on her terminal before the body was found confirm Marta knew Viktor was about to file a report that would trigger a formal investigation into her clearance work.

clues.heading: The Unlocked Crane Cab
clues.text: Dirk Houten's crane cab — which should have been secured during his meal break per terminal safety protocol — was found unlocked when investigators checked it. The crane's internal log shows the cab was accessed at 22:15, during the meal break window, for four minutes. Dirk claims he never left the meal room.
clues.alone: Dirk accessed his crane cab during his meal break and denies it — four minutes of unaccounted activity at the crane.

links.insight: The cargo hook from Dirk's crane at the murder scene and his crane cab accessed during the meal break confirm Dirk removed the hook from the cab during those four minutes — placing the weapon at the container before the killing occurred.

clues.heading: Marta at the Container
clues.text: A security camera at the entrance to Container Row 7 captures a figure matching Marta Beel's build and high-visibility vest entering the row at 22:28. The camera angle does not show the container itself. Marta claims she was in the quayside office the entire evening and did not visit Row 7.
clues.alone: A figure matching Marta entered Container Row 7 at 22:28 — during the window of death — contradicting her claimed alibi.

links.insight: Viktor struck from the left with no struggle and Marta's confirmed presence in Container Row 7 during the murder window confirm she approached Viktor from his left while he faced the open door — he had no reason to be wary of her arrival.

clues.heading: Dirk's Meal Room Lie
clues.text: The meal room's electronic entry log shows Dirk Houten badged out at 22:12 and did not badge back in until 22:31 — a nineteen-minute gap he claims was spent in the room the entire time. The meal room requires a badge swipe to enter and exit. His badge log contradicts his statement.
clues.alone: Dirk left the meal room for nineteen minutes during the murder window — his alibi is physically impossible.

links.insight: Sven confirmed at Row 9 during the murder window and Dirk's badge log showing he left the meal room for nineteen minutes confirm Dirk was unaccounted for during the exact period when the weapon was moved and Viktor was killed.

clues.heading: The Draft Notification
clues.text: A port authority incident notification — draft, unsent — was found open on Marta Beel's terminal when investigators arrived. The draft cited inspector interference with legitimate clearance documentation and named Viktor Haus as the subject. It was timestamped 22:19 — nine minutes before the security camera placed her in Row 7.
clues.alone: Marta was drafting a complaint against Viktor at 22:19 — then headed to Row 7 at 22:28 — suggesting she went to confront him directly rather than filing through channels.

links.insight: Viktor's flagged discrepancy in Marta's manifest and her draft complaint against him timestamped before she walked to his location confirm she went to Container 7-Delta to stop his report — the killing followed the confrontation she initiated.

rookie.epilogue: Marta Beel walked to Container 7-Delta with the intention of talking Viktor out of filing his discrepancy report. She found him facing the open door, reviewing his notes. She picked up the cargo hook Dirk had left on the container floor — he'd moved it from his crane cab during his unlogged break, intending to use it for an off-book lift later that night. One opportunistic blow and Viktor was dead. Marta had not planned it. Dirk had not known. The hook was simply there. Both were arrested — Marta for the killing, Dirk as an accessory after the fact when it emerged he'd helped cover the hook's movement.

detective.title: Dead Weight
detective.flavor: Viktor Haus found a two-crate discrepancy and ended up dead over it. Marta Beel killed him. But Dirk Houten moved the weapon before either of them arrived — he just didn't know what it would be used for. Two separate plans collided in Container 7-Delta and only one person survived.
detective.emptyState: Two plans. One container. Find how both of them got in.

items.name: Dirk Houten
items.detail: Crane operator — moved the weapon unknowingly, meal room alibi false.

items.name: Marta Beel
items.detail: Customs officer — camera places her at Row 7, manifest discrepancy motive.

items.name: Sven Laak
items.detail: Maintenance worker — Row 9 alibi confirmed, hydraulic fluid covered.

items.name: Pieter Vos
items.detail: Shift supervisor — assigned Viktor's inspection, signed the night's work orders.

items.name: Container 7-Delta
items.detail: Primary crime scene — cargo hook, Viktor's body, no lock damage.

items.name: The Quayside Office
items.detail: Marta's claimed location — draft complaint found on terminal here.

items.name: The Maintenance Bay
items.detail: Sven's confirmed work location — alibi holds.

items.name: Crane 7 Cab
items.detail: Dirk's crane — accessed during meal break, hook removed.

items.name: Steel Cargo Hook
items.detail: From Crane 7 — moved to container before the killing.

items.name: Packing Foam Block
items.detail: In the container — no blood or impact trace found on any block.

items.name: Manifest Clipboard
items.detail: Found wiped near the door — Marta's fingerprints on the clip.

items.name: Hydraulic Wrench
items.detail: Sven's tool — accounted for in the maintenance log, returned intact.

items.name: Clearance Override
items.detail: Customs officers can unlock any container under active review.

items.name: Master Keycard
items.detail: Shift supervisors hold a master keycard for all terminal locks.

items.name: Unlocked Door
items.detail: Container 7-Delta was found already open — Viktor had unlocked it.

items.name: Crane Access Route
items.detail: Crane operators use elevated walkways bypassing ground-level checkpoints.

clues.heading: The Hook's Journey
clues.text: The cargo hook from Crane 7 was logged as secured in the cab at the start of the shift. The crane's internal sensor log shows the hook locker was opened at 22:15 — during Dirk's meal break — and was not re-secured. The hook was found inside Container 7-Delta, thirty metres from the crane. Someone carried it there.
clues.alone: The hook was removed from the crane during Dirk's meal break and carried to Container 7-Delta — Dirk is the only person with crane cab access.

links.insight: The hook removed from the crane at 22:15 and the cargo manifest showing an off-book lift was planned for Container 7-Delta later that night confirm Dirk moved the hook for his own unauthorised purpose — not knowing it would become the murder weapon.

clues.heading: Fingerprints on the Hook
clues.text: The steel cargo hook carries two sets of fingerprints: Dirk Houten's, consistent with routine crane handling, and a partial set in blood — too smeared for identification but consistent with a smaller hand. Marta Beel's hands are significantly smaller than Dirk's. The blood partial is on the grip, not the shackle — it was held, not just touched.
clues.alone: Two people handled the hook — Dirk during routine use and a second person with smaller hands during or after the killing.

links.insight: The blood partial on the hook consistent with a smaller hand and the security camera placing Marta in Container Row 7 during the murder window confirm Marta picked up and used the hook that Dirk had inadvertently left at the scene.

clues.heading: Sven Cleared, Pieter Questioned
clues.text: Sven Laak is definitively cleared — the forklift driver's sighting at 22:30, the maintenance log, and the returned torque wrench account for his entire evening. Shift supervisor Pieter Vos, however, cannot account for fifteen minutes between 22:20 and 22:35 — he claims he was doing a visual check of the south quay but no camera confirms this.
clues.alone: Sven is fully cleared. Pieter Vos has an unconfirmed gap during the murder window — but no physical evidence places him at Container 7-Delta.

links.insight: Sven cleared completely and Pieter's unconfirmed gap lacking any physical evidence at the scene together narrow the field to Marta and Dirk — one who moved the weapon and one who used it.

clues.heading: The Two-Crate Gap
clues.text: Viktor's inspection report identifies a two-crate shortfall in Container 7-Delta — the manifest was cleared by Marta Beel six hours earlier. Port authority protocol requires an automatic audit when a discrepancy exceeds one unit. Two missing crates would trigger a formal customs investigation and a review of all clearances Marta had processed that quarter.
clues.alone: Marta's clearance work faced a formal investigation the moment Viktor filed his report — two crates was enough to trigger a quarter-wide audit of her work.

links.insight: The two-crate discrepancy triggering a quarter-wide audit and Marta's draft complaint drafted nine minutes before she walked to Viktor's location confirm she understood the stakes and chose to confront him rather than let the report proceed.

clues.heading: The Off-Book Lift
clues.text: A handwritten note found in Dirk's crane cab — not an official work order — reads 7D after 23:00, Henk's load, cash. Container 7-Delta, after 23:00, for someone called Henk. An off-book cargo move, bypassing customs clearance. Dirk had moved the hook to the container in preparation for this job — not for Viktor.
clues.alone: Dirk was planning an off-book cargo lift from Container 7-Delta after 23:00 — he moved the hook there for his own unauthorised job, not to commit murder.

links.insight: The hook removed from the crane at 22:15 and the off-book lift note in Dirk's cab confirm he placed the weapon at the scene entirely by accident — the hook was there for his cash job, not for a killing.

clues.heading: The Camera and the Vest
clues.text: The security camera at Row 7's entrance captures the figure at 22:28 in a customs high-visibility vest — orange with a blue stripe, the specific pattern issued to clearance officers. Port workers wear yellow. Maintenance staff wear green. The figure is a customs officer. Only Marta Beel was assigned to the 7-series row that evening.
clues.alone: The figure at Row 7 was wearing a customs vest — the specific type issued to clearance officers. Marta was the only customs officer assigned to that section.

links.insight: The blood partial on the hook consistent with a smaller hand and the customs vest identifying the figure as Marta confirm she entered the container, found the hook already there, and used it when the confrontation with Viktor escalated.

clues.heading: Access to the Container
clues.text: Container 7-Delta was under active customs review — meaning Marta Beel held clearance override authority to unlock it at any time without logging the access. Viktor had unlocked it for his inspection and left it open. Marta did not need to force entry or use any key — the door was standing open when she arrived.
clues.alone: Marta had legal authority to access the container and Viktor had already left the door open — her entry required no special action and left no access record.

links.insight: Sven cleared completely and Marta's unlogged clearance override access to the container confirm she could enter without any record — her presence in the container is evidenced only by the camera and the fingerprint partial, not an access log.

clues.heading: The Draft and the Walk
clues.text: Marta's unsent draft complaint was timestamped 22:19. The security camera places her at Row 7 at 22:28 — a nine-minute gap. The terminal's internal layout means the walk from the quayside office to Row 7 takes eight to ten minutes at normal pace. She left almost immediately after drafting the complaint.
clues.alone: Marta drafted the complaint, then walked directly to Viktor's location — the timing confirms she abandoned the official channel and chose direct confrontation.

links.insight: The two-crate discrepancy triggering a quarter-wide audit of Marta's work and her immediate walk to Viktor's container after drafting a complaint confirm she understood filing would not stop him and decided to confront him personally instead.

detective.epilogue: Marta Beel had clearance override authority and Viktor had left the door open. She walked in, found him reviewing his notes, and picked up the cargo hook sitting on the container floor — placed there by Dirk for a completely unrelated cash job. The argument lasted less than two minutes. The hook was simply the nearest heavy object. Dirk's off-book lift was discovered when investigators found his note. He was charged as an accessory after the fact, having helped Marta move the hook out of the container after realising what had happened. He had not planned any of it. Neither had she, really.

inspector.title: Dead Weight
inspector.flavor: Marta killed Viktor. Dirk moved the weapon. But the two-crate discrepancy Viktor found wasn't Marta's mistake — it was deliberate. She had been systematically misdeclaring cargo for eighteen months on behalf of a third party. Viktor's report threatened not just her job but an entire network. And Dirk knew more about that network than his off-book lift note suggested.
inspector.emptyState: Marta killed. Dirk helped. Find out who they were both working for.

items.name: Marta Beel
items.detail: The killer — clearance override, cargo hook, confrontation gone fatal.

items.name: Dirk Houten
items.detail: Moved the weapon — cash job for Henk, helped after the fact.

items.name: Sven Laak
items.detail: Fully cleared — Row 9, hydraulic fluid, torque wrench returned.

items.name: Pieter Vos
items.detail: Shift supervisor — unconfirmed gap, used by Baas to delay radio contact.

items.name: Henrik Baas
items.detail: Port freight broker — Dirk's Henk, Marta's eighteen-month client.

items.name: Container 7-Delta
items.detail: Primary crime scene — open door, cargo hook, no access log.

items.name: The Quayside Office
items.detail: Marta's terminal — draft complaint, clearance records, phone log.

items.name: Crane 7 Cab
items.detail: Dirk's crane — hook locker opened 22:15, off-book note found.

items.name: The Port Authority Archive
items.detail: Where eighteen months of misdeclaration records are stored.

items.name: Henrik Baas's Freight Office
items.detail: Off-site office — payment records, container assignments, phone logs.

items.name: Steel Cargo Hook
items.detail: From Crane 7 — Dirk moved it, Marta used it, both handled it after.

items.name: Packing Foam Block
items.detail: No trace evidence — eliminated.

items.name: Manifest Clipboard
items.detail: Marta's fingerprints on the clip — she brought it into the container.

items.name: Hydraulic Wrench
items.detail: Sven's tool — accounted for, cleared.

items.name: Viktor's Own Torch
items.detail: Found switched on near the body — dropped when struck, not a weapon.

items.name: Clearance Override
items.detail: Marta's customs authority — no log, no key required.

items.name: Master Keycard
items.detail: Pieter Vos holds the only master keycard — not used that evening.

items.name: Unlocked Door
items.detail: Viktor left it open — Marta walked straight in.

items.name: Crane Access Route
items.detail: Elevated walkway bypasses ground checkpoints — Dirk's usual path.

items.name: Broker's Terminal Pass
items.detail: Henrik Baas held a visitor pass granting quay access after hours.

items.name: Protect the Misdeclaration Scheme
items.detail: Stop Viktor exposing eighteen months of deliberate cargo fraud.

items.name: Silence a Witness
items.detail: Viktor had seen something beyond the manifest discrepancy.

items.name: Prevent Personal Ruin
items.detail: An audit would end Marta's career and trigger criminal charges.

items.name: Protect a Third Party
items.detail: The scheme served Henrik Baas — Marta killed to protect him too.

items.name: Cover a Prior Crime
items.detail: The misdeclarations concealed an earlier offence now at risk of exposure.

clues.heading: Eighteen Months of Gaps
clues.text: A port authority audit of Marta Beel's clearance records — triggered by Viktor's draft report — reveals forty-three instances of minor cargo misdeclaration over eighteen months, all on containers assigned to freight broker Henrik Baas. Each instance involves one or two units underdeclared. The pattern is systematic, not accidental.
clues.alone: Marta had been deliberately misdeclaring Henrik Baas's cargo for eighteen months — forty-three times, always minor enough to avoid automatic audit triggers.

links.insight: Marta's systematic misdeclaration of Henrik Baas's cargo over eighteen months and the payment records showing regular deposits from a shell account linked to Baas confirm she was financially compensated for the fraud — this was a deliberate commercial arrangement, not negligence.

clues.heading: The Phone Call Before the Walk
clues.text: Marta Beel's work mobile — recovered and imaged — shows a two-minute call received at 22:17, two minutes before she drafted the complaint. The caller ID is listed as H.B. The call ended at 22:19. The complaint draft began at 22:19. She walked to Row 7 at 22:28. The call preceded every subsequent action.
clues.alone: Marta received a two-minute call from H.B. — Henrik Baas — immediately before drafting the complaint and walking to Viktor's container.

links.insight: The call from Henrik Baas immediately before Marta's walk to Viktor's location and Baas's visitor pass records showing he was on the quay that evening confirm Baas directed Marta to deal with Viktor — he was present and coordinating.

clues.heading: Dirk's Arrangement with Baas
clues.text: The handwritten note in Dirk's cab reads 7D after 23:00, Henk's load, cash. Port records show Henrik Baas — Henk — had a container assignment change that placed his cargo in 7-Delta two days prior. Dirk had been moving off-book loads for Baas for at least six months, evidenced by three prior cash deposits to Dirk's account matching the dates of unexplained crane movements.
clues.alone: Dirk had been running off-book lifts for Henrik Baas for six months — the cash job that night was part of an ongoing arrangement, not a one-off.

links.insight: Dirk's six-month history of off-book lifts for Baas and Marta's eighteen-month misdeclaration record for Baas confirm both port workers were independently employed by the same freight broker in a coordinated scheme to move cargo below customs scrutiny.

clues.heading: Baas on the Quay
clues.text: Henrik Baas held a freight broker's visitor pass granting after-hours quay access. Terminal entry records show his pass was used at 21:58 that evening — thirty minutes before Viktor's estimated time of death. Exit records show the pass was not used to leave until 23:15. Baas was on the quay during the entire murder window.
clues.alone: Henrik Baas was physically present on the quay from 21:58 until 23:15 — he was there before, during, and after Viktor's death.

links.insight: Baas present on the quay during the murder window and his terminal access showing he visited the freight management system at 22:14 confirm Baas was actively managing both operatives simultaneously that evening.

clues.heading: The Shell Account Payments
clues.text: Financial records obtained from Dutch banking authorities show regular monthly transfers of 800 euros to Marta Beel's personal account from a shell company registered in Luxembourg. The shell company's beneficial owner is Henrik Baas. The payments began eighteen months ago — exactly when the misdeclarations started.
clues.alone: Marta was paid 800 euros monthly by Henrik Baas's shell company for eighteen months — the payments align precisely with the start of the misdeclaration pattern.

links.insight: Marta's forty-three systematic misdeclarations for Baas's cargo and the matching monthly payments from his shell company confirm she was knowingly operating as a paid corrupt customs officer within his freight network.

clues.heading: Baas's Terminal Access
clues.text: Terminal records show Henrik Baas's visitor pass accessed the freight management terminal at 22:14 — one minute before Dirk opened the crane hook locker. The terminal allows freight brokers to reassign container priorities. At 22:14 Baas moved Container 7-Delta to the top of the night's clearance queue — ensuring Viktor's inspection would conclude there.
clues.alone: Baas manipulated the container clearance queue at 22:14 to ensure Viktor ended up at 7-Delta — he directed Viktor to the location where Marta and Dirk's activities converged.

links.insight: The call from Baas to Marta at 22:17 and his terminal manipulation at 22:14 confirm he was actively coordinating the situation — he pushed Viktor to the container, then called Marta to deal with the consequences.

clues.heading: Dirk's Cash Deposits
clues.text: Dirk Houten's bank account shows six cash deposits over the past six months ranging from 300 to 600 euros, each following a night shift with an unexplained crane movement in the terminal log. The deposits match dates when Henrik Baas had containers at the terminal. Dirk was being paid for the same off-book network as Marta — neither knew the other was involved.
clues.alone: Dirk was paid by Baas for off-book crane work six times — he and Marta were both corrupt employees of the same network without knowing about each other.

links.insight: Dirk's six months of cash deposits from Baas's network and the off-book lift note in his cab confirm both Dirk and Marta were independently corrupt employees of Henrik Baas — their collision in Container 7-Delta was an accident Baas had inadvertently created.

clues.heading: Pieter Vos's Gap Explained
clues.text: Shift supervisor Pieter Vos's unconfirmed fifteen-minute gap is explained when CCTV from the south quay — a camera previously overlooked — shows him speaking to Henrik Baas at 22:22. The conversation lasts approximately four minutes. Vos admits under questioning that Baas asked him to delay calling Viktor on the radio — Vos assumed it was a routine scheduling request.
clues.alone: Pieter Vos was with Henrik Baas at 22:22 — Baas used him to delay Viktor's radio contact, buying time for Marta to reach the container first.

links.insight: Baas present on the quay during the murder window and his use of Pieter Vos to delay Viktor's radio contact confirm Baas was actively managing the situation in real time — coordinating Marta, Dirk, and Pieter simultaneously without any of them knowing the full picture.

inspector.epilogue: Henrik Baas had built a tidy little network: Marta cleared his containers with minor underdeclarations, Dirk moved the off-book loads after hours, and Pieter occasionally smoothed scheduling. None of them knew about the others. When Viktor found the two-crate gap, Baas called Marta, manipulated the queue to keep Viktor at 7-Delta, and used Pieter to delay the radio check. Marta went to stop Viktor. Dirk's hook was already there. Marta swung it. Baas left the quay at 23:15 having said nothing incriminating to anyone. He was arrested four months later when the shell company payments surfaced. Marta received twelve years. Dirk received three as accessory. Pieter was cautioned. Baas received seven years for conspiracy. The two missing crates were never found.
